steps.title: Identify the contribution
steps.content: The passage says Danielson and Voss's unique contribution was reconstructing the earliest extant annual fiscal records for any sovereign state. Those records showed Philip II's defaults came from short-term cash shortages rather than unsustainable long-term debts.

steps.title: Reject narrower claims
steps.content: Choice A is too narrow and mainly contrasts them with Hartman and Nystrom. Choice C gives a specific cause not stated in the passage. Choice D says they added nuance to earlier findings, but the passage emphasizes their own evidence and insight. The answer is B.
